title: Neolloydia mariposensis
intro: Neolloydia mariposensis, also known as the Mariposa Pincushion, is a type of cactus native to the southwestern United States. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as wound healing, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. The bioactive constituents of Neolloydia mariposensis include flavonoids, saponins, and glycosides, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, tinctures, and salves, which are often used to treat skin irritations, wounds, and other minor health issues.
taxonomy: Neolloydia mariposensis is classified within the Cactaceae family, specifically within the subfamily Mammillioideae. This plant is a species of cactus native to Mexico. The classification also places it within the genus Neolloydia, which is a group of small, globular cacti. Neolloydia mariposensis is further classified as a species within this genus due to its unique characteristics and distinct features. Its classification is closely related to that of other species within the Mammillioideae subfamily.
